title: The Week Altruist Shook the Markets
executive_summary: Beim argues the Altruist/Hazel announcement triggered a $130 billion market sell-off not because AI is new to wealth management, but because Hazel is integrated directly into the custodial system — giving it live access to account data that legacy platforms cannot replicate without rebuilding from scratch

The core mechanism is the conversion of high-skill, low-scale labour into software leverage: tax planning that previously required a specialist and several hours now runs in minutes across an entire advisor book
Legacy custodians face a structural disadvantage — they cannot bolt AI onto decades-old infrastructure and achieve the same depth of integration that native platforms have by design
The market's reaction was rational: even modest AI-driven productivity gains compound across a trillion-dollar industry measured in basis points, justifying large valuation shifts

Beim concludes that AI's value in wealth management will not come from replacing advisors but from radically increasing their leverage — the question for incumbents is whether they can build that leverage before their clients move to those who already have it
the_one_insight: The competitive moat in AI wealth management is not the AI model — it is the data layer. Hazel is different because it sits inside the custodial system. That is an architectural advantage legacy platforms cannot close by adding a chatbot.

key_quotes.text: Hazel is different for one reason that matters enormously: it is integrated directly into the custodial system and has access to custodial data.
key_quotes.context: On why Altruist's architecture creates a structural advantage that legacy competitors cannot easily replicate

key_quotes.text: It converts high-skill, low-scale labor into software leverage.
key_quotes.context: On the economic mechanism behind AI-powered tax planning

key_quotes.text: AI's value in wealth management will not come from replacing advisors, but from radically increasing their leverage.
key_quotes.context: On how the wealth management industry should frame the AI opportunity
